# Financial Services - Full Documentation

# Anypoint MQ Setup Guide

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The Postman collection `Anypoint-MQ-Configuration` can be used to pre-create all Anypoint MQ
destinations required by the accelerator components. This collection can be found in the
[FINS Common Resources project](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/fins-common-resources-src/).

## Environment Settings

The collection requires the `dev` environment to be created and assigned proper values reflecting
your target Anypoint org before running any of the requests. A template for this environment, which
can be imported directly into your Postman workspace, has also been provided in the FINS Common
Resources project.

## Running the Collection

The collection itself defines variables that must be overridden prior to running any requests. The
contents of the collection have been organized into separate folders, which should be executed in
the following order:

1.  Authorization
2.  Client-Management (optional)
3.  Queue-Management
4.  Exchange-Management

Requests in the `Verification` folder can be run to validate the results of the above operations. If
desired, objects created by the collection can be cleaned up by running requests found in the
`Deletions` folder.

## Verify Destinations

The following MQ destinations will be created on successful execution.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Queue Name</th><th>Bound to Exchange</th><th>Dead Letter Queue Name</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>accelerator-notification-queue</p></td><td><p>accelerator-notification-exchange</p></td><td><p>accelerator-notification-dl-queue</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fins-account-update-queue</p></td><td><p>fins-account-update-exchange</p></td><td><p>fins-account-update-dl-queue</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fins-ach-inbound-queue</p></td><td><p>fins-ach-inbound-exchange</p></td><td><p>fins-ach-inbound-dl-queue</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fins-ach-outbound-queue</p></td><td><p>fins-ach-outbound-exchange</p></td><td><p>fins-ach-outbound-dl-queue</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fins-address-update-queue</p></td><td><p>fins-address-update-exchange</p></td><td><p>fins-address-update-dl-queue</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fins-card-update-queue</p></td><td><p>fins-card-update-exchange</p></td><td><p>fins-card-update-dl-queue</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fins-fedwire-inbound-queue</p></td><td><p>fins-fedwire-inbound-exchange</p></td><td><p>fins-fedwire-inbound-dl-queue</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fins-fedwire-outbound-queue</p></td><td><p>fins-fedwire-outbound-exchange</p></td><td><p>fins-fedwire-outbound-dl-queue</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fins-lead-update-queue</p></td><td><p>fins-lead-update-exchange</p></td><td><p>fins-address-update-dl-queue</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fins-loan-document-update-queue</p></td><td><p>fins-document-update-exchange</p></td><td><p>fins-loan-document-update-dl-queue</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fins-opportunity-update-queue</p></td><td><p>fins-opportunity-update-exchange</p></td><td><p>fins-opportunity-update-dl-queue</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fins-party-update-queue</p></td><td><p>fins-party-update-exchange</p></td><td><p>fins-party-update-dl-queue</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fins-partyrole-update-queue</p></td><td><p>fins-partyrole-update-exchange</p></td><td><p>fins-partyrole-update-dl-queue</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fins-payments-webui-queue</p></td><td><p>fins-payment-notice-exchange</p></td><td><p>fins-payments-webui-dl-queue</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fins-reconciliation-queue</p></td><td><p>fins-reconciliation-exchange</p></td><td><p>fins-reconciliation-dl-queue</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fins-swift-inbound-queue</p></td><td><p>fins-swift-inbound-exchange</p></td><td><p>fins-swift-inbound-dl-queue</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fins-swift-outbound-queue</p></td><td><p>fins-swift-outbound-exchange</p></td><td><p>fins-swift-outbound-dl-queue</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fins-transaction-update-queue</p></td><td><p>fins-transaction-update-exchange</p></td><td><p>fins-transaction-update-dl-queue</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fins-wealth-document-update-queue</p></td><td><p>fins-document-update-exchange</p></td><td><p>fins-wealth-document-update-dl-queue</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Note that the `accelerator-notification` destinations may already exist in your target environment.

## See Also

- [Prerequisites](prerequisites)
- [MuleSoft Accelerator for Financial Services](./)
---

# MuleSoft Accelerator for Financial Services

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

MuleSoft Accelerator for Financial Services simplifies customer 360 initiatives by unlocking both
core banking and insurance platforms, systems of engagement (such as CRM and ITSM), Insuretechs, and
FinTech data. This enables a higher level of customer service, real-time relationship management,
and a more connected digital experience.

## Use Cases

### Core Banking Foundation

The Core Banking Foundation use case enables a 360 view of your customers' financial information.
There are two variations of this use case: one to jumpstart the unlocking of any core, and one to
unlock a specific core.

- [Unlock any core](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/mulesoft-accelerator-for-financial-services/minor/1.12/pages/a5h-00q/Use%20case%201a%20-%20Unlock%20any%20core/) -
  Unlock and display financial summaries from multiple systems.
- [Unlock Jack Henry SilverLake core](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/mulesoft-accelerator-for-financial-services/minor/1.12/pages/32n-se5/Use%20case%201b%20-%20Unlock%20Jack%20Henry%20SilverLake%20core/) -
  Accelerate the delivery of your customer 360 initiatives by unlocking your Jack Henry SilverLake
  core.
- [Unlock Thought Machine Vault Core](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/mulesoft-accelerator-for-financial-services/minor/1.12/pages/n7d-jvs/Use%20case%201c%20-%20Unlock%20Thought%20Machine%20Vault%20Core/) -
  Accelerate the delivery of your customer 360 initiatives by unlocking your Thought Machine Vault
  Core.

### Onboarding

This use case accelerates the delivery and development of an enhanced onboarding solution to
streamline banking loan origination and wealth management client account opening procedures. There
are two extensions for this use case:

- [Customer Onboarding - Banking](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/mulesoft-accelerator-for-financial-services/minor/1.12/pages/Use%20case%202a%20-%20Customer%20onboarding%20-%20Banking/) -
  Create an easy and straightforward onboarding experience by empowering consumers to apply for a
  loan via a digital channel.
- [Client Onboarding - Wealth management](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/mulesoft-accelerator-for-financial-services/minor/1.12/pages/Use%20case%202b%20-%20Client%20onboarding%20-%20Wealth%20management/) -
  Accelerate the delivery and development of a streamlined and automated client onboarding solution.

### Payments Modernization

The
[Payments Modernization](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/mulesoft-accelerator-for-financial-services/minor/1.12/pages/Use%20case%203%20-%20Payments%20modernization/)
use case accelerates the development of modern payment solutions for Financial Institutions. Banks
and Credit Unions will be able to deliver a diverse payments landscape that can address multiple
types of financial transactions and customer needs.

### Address Change Orchestration

This use case accelerates the delivery and development of a home address validation solution for
streamlining downstream processes impacted by postal address changes. There are two extensions for
this use case:

- [Address Change Orchestration: Insurance](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/mulesoft-accelerator-for-financial-services/minor/1.12/pages/Use%20case%204a%20-%20Address%20change%20orchestration%20-%20Insurance/) -
  Streamline downstream events that result from policyholder or carrier postal address adjustments.
- [Address Change Orchestration: Banking](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/mulesoft-accelerator-for-financial-services/minor/1.12/pages/Use%20case%204b%20-%20Address%20change%20orchestration%20-%20Banking/) -
  Enhance customer service teams to upsell, cross-sell, and service opportunities that result from
  customer postal code address changes.

### Optimize Customer Experiences

The
[Optimize Customer Experiences with Salesforce CDP](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/mulesoft-accelerator-for-financial-services/minor/1.12/pages/dcb-oxw/Use%20case%205%20-%20Optimize%20customer%20experiences%20with%20Data%20Cloud/)
use case provides support for ingesting data from multiple sources into Data Cloud and then using
the results of Data Cloud activations to optimize customer experiences by providing Customer Service
Representatives (CSRs) with marketing insights.

### Salesforce Org Sync

The
[Salesforce Org Sync](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/mulesoft-accelerator-for-financial-services/minor/1.12/pages/0pg-v87/Use%20case%206%20-%20Salesforce%20org%20sync/)
use case enables financial institutions using a multi-org strategy to sync customer data across
Salesforce Financial Services Cloud, Sales Cloud, and Service Cloud orgs.

### Unlock AMS Through Automation for Insurance Brokers

The
[Unlock AMS Through Automation for Insurance Brokers](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/mulesoft-accelerator-for-financial-services/minor/1.12/pages/3cn-8tr/Use%20case%207%20-%20Unlock%20AMS%20through%20automation%20for%20insurance%20brokers/)
use case enables insurance brokers to view policy data from an Agency Management System (AMS)
platform in their CRM platform.

### Additional Assets

The following **use-case agnostic** assets are also available:

- [BIAN assets](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/mulesoft-accelerator-for-financial-services/minor/1.12/pages/BIAN%20assets/) -
  Jumpstart your BIAN standard implementation using these BIAN-compliant assets, including API specs
  and implementation templates.

## See Also

- [Getting Started Guide](../../accelerators-home/getting-started)
- [Prerequisites](prerequisites)
- [Release Notes](../../release-notes/accelerators/financial-services/financial-services-release-notes)
- [Upgrade Notes](upgrade-notes)
- [MuleSoft Accelerator for Financial Services on Exchange](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/mulesoft-accelerator-for-financial-services/)
---

# Payments Modernization Demo Setup Guide

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

This guide provides instructions for setting up all the components required to run the Payments
Modernization use case demo.

## Summary

- A Docker runtime will be required to deploy containers needed by the solution
- A generic core banking system will be used as the primary system of record
- Moov.io containers will be used to simulate the processing of ACH and Fedwire messages
- A third-party Java library will be used to simulate the processing of SWIFT messages
- A custom process will be used to simulate incoming payment confirmations
- A
  [Payments Front-end Web UI](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/fins-payments-frontend-webui-src/)
  has been built to demonstrate the functionality implemented by this use case
- MultiChain Wallet Node is used to support processing of BlockChain Transactions

## Setup

This guide sets up a demo environment with the supported backend services used by the provided API
implementations. When these APIs are adopted by a MuleSoft customer, it is expected that the
customer will replace these example backend services with their preferred services.

The following containerized services are deployed on docker. For demo purposes, it is recommended to
deploy all containers on a single Docker node.

- moov.io ACH service
- moov.io Fedwire service
- [Payments Front-end Web UI](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/fins-payments-frontend-webui-src/)
- Blockchain based on [MultiChain](https://www.multichain.com/)

The
[Payments Front-end Web UI](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/fins-payments-frontend-webui-src/)
can also be run locally, outside of a container, by following the instructions provided in its
README file.

**Payments Front-end Web UI recommended setup:**

1.  Install Docker and its dependencies on the server of your choice.
2.  Configure the
    [Payments Front-end Web UI](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/fins-payments-frontend-webui-src/)
    by modifying the following properties in the .env file:
    - `REACT_APP_PAYMENTS_WEBUI_EXP_API_ENDPOINT` should point to an instance of the
      [Payments Web UI Experience API](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/fins-payments-webui-exp-api-spec/)
    - `REACT_APP_PAYMENTS_WEBUI_EXP_API_WEBSOCKET_ENDPOINT` should point to an instance of the
      [Payments Web UI Experience API](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/fins-payments-webui-exp-api-spec/)

3.  Build the docker image by running its docker-build.sh script.
4.  Start the docker container by running the docker-run.sh script on the machine running the Docker
    server, or use a Docker administration UI to manage the containers.
5.  Update `https.cors.allow-url` property in the
    [Payments Web UI Experience API](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/fins-payments-webui-exp-api/)
    with the Payments Front-end Web UI endpoint to allow CORS.

**Moov.io recommended setup:**

The moov.io images are provided pre-built as-is. They can be started on the Docker server by using
the included docker-run.sh scripts.

**MultiChain setup:**

To run the MultiChain node as a docker container, perform the following steps:

1.  Use the Dockerfile from the scripts folder in the
    [Blockchain System API](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/fins-blockchain-sys-api-spec/)
    to build the MultiChain image.
2.  Run the `docker-run.sh` script to deploy the container and setup network ports. Run
    `docker-exec.sh` to create MultiChain and configure the json-rpc service to interact with the
    MultiChain container.

## See Also

- [Prerequisites](prerequisites)
- [MuleSoft Accelerator for Financial Services](./)
---

# Prerequisites for MuleSoft Accelerator for Financial Services

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

This page provides a summary of the guides available for configuring the various systems and
products required to implement the use cases provided by this solution. The individual systems
needed, and amount of configuration required, depend on which assets are to be deployed in your
target environment(s) - refer to the use case pages for more details.

## Available Guides

The following setup and configuration guides are available for this accelerator:

- [Anypoint MQ setup guide](anypointmq-setup-guide)
- [Payments modernization demo setup guide](payments-setup-guide)
- [RPA Setup Guide](rpa-setup-guide)
- [Optimize Customer Experiences Use Case Setup Guide](sfdc-data-cloud-setup-guide)
- [Salesforce Financial Services Cloud setup guide](sfdc-fsc-setup-guide)
- [Salesforce Marketing Cloud setup guide](sfdc-mc-setup-guide)

Most of the implementation templates also contain details on requirements specific to those
applications.

## See Also

- [Getting Started Guide](../../accelerators-home/getting-started)
- [MuleSoft Accelerator for Financial Services](./)
---

# RPA Setup Guide

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

This guide provides instructions for implementing the
[Unlock AMS Through Automation for Insurance Brokers](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/mulesoft-accelerator-for-financial-services/minor/1.12/pages/3cn-8tr/Use%20case%207%20-%20Unlock%20AMS%20through%20automation%20for%20insurance%20brokers/)
use case, which is part of the MuleSoft Accelerator for Financial Services.

The use case leverages MuleSoftâs Robotic Process Automation (RPA) solution to retrieve customer
data from your Agency Management System (AMS) and automatically load the data into Salesforce
Financial Services Cloud (FSC). To implement this use case, you must set up RPA on the device that
runs the automation process and configure the solution to your specific AMS.

## Before You Begin

- Ensure that the Anypoint Platform account to use has
  [the RPA Automations Manager and RPA Bots Manager permissions](../../rpa-home/automation-userrolesandpermissions-anypointrpapermissions).
- Install the latest version of MuleSoft
  [RPA Builder](../../rpa-builder/latest/install-and-configure) and
  [RPA Bot](../../rpa-bot/latest/installation) from the RPA Manager home page.
- Ensure that you have access to your AMS and its database metadata. Configure the custom fields in
  the \[FINS AMS Salesforce Sync Handler - Implementation Template\], ensuring that the custom field
  names match the column names on the CSV file.
- Ensure that you have access to the Salesforce Financial Services Cloud organization to which your
  AMS data will be loaded.
- Download the `AMS_to_FSC.crpa` file to the same device on which RPA Builder is installed.

## Workflow Overview

1.  Create two projects in RPA Manager:
    - The first project contains the process for getting all relevant datasets and looping over them
      in your AMS. For each dataset, the second project is called.
    - The second project contains the process for getting the data from one AMS dataset and adding
      it to a CSV file, which is imported at a later step to FSC.

2.  Import and adapt the CRPA file in the second project in RPA Builder.
3.  Publish the second project to Anypoint Exchange in RPA Manager.
4.  Build the process for the first project from scratch in RPA Builder.
5.  Deploy a scheduled configuration for the first project.

## Create a Project in RPA Manager

To create a new project in RPA Manager, see
[Create an Automation Project](../../rpa-manager/processautomation-manage#create-an-automation-project).
If a new category is needed when creating a project, see
[Create a Category](../../rpa-manager/processautomation-prepare-project-category#create-a-category).

Once you create the project,
[move it directly to the Build phase](../../rpa-manager/processautomation-develop#build-a-process)
without making any changes.

## Import the CRPA File to RPA Builder

1.  Log in to RPA Builder on the device on which the process runs.
2.  On the [Repository tab in the Explorer pane](../../rpa-builder/latest/ui-overview) in RPA
    Builder, click the name of the process you created.
3.  Click **File** on the [Menu Bar](../../rpa-builder/latest/ui-overview#menu-bar), and then click
    **Import Project**.
4.  Select the CRPA file you downloaded from Exchange. The project opens in the model pane and
    includes three activities, which you must adapt to your AMS.

## Adapt the CRPA File to Your AMS

1.  Double-click each activity on the model pane to open the workflow.
2.  Compare the displayed process with your process and adapt the action steps accordingly. See
    [Example of Adapting the CRPA File](#example-of-adapting-the-crpa-file) for a detailed example.
3.  Save and upload the project to RPA Manager.

## Transfer the Second Project to the Test Phase and Test It

Now that you have adapted the file to your AMS,
[transfer the project to the Test phase and test it](../../rpa-manager/processautomation-deploy#test-a-process-automation).

## Transfer the Second Project to the Production Phase

After successfully testing the project, transfer the project to the Production phase and publish the
project to Anypoint Exchange
[as an invokable run configuration](../../rpa-manager/processautomation-deploy#publish-automation-exchange).
For help with this process, refer to:

- [Connecting to RPA Processes from Salesforce Products](../../rpa-manager/settings-connect)
- [Use Flows with MuleSoft RPA](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.flow_build_use_flows_with_mulesoft_rpa.htm&type=5)

## Implement and Deploy the First Project

Now that you have published the second project as an invokable run configuration, you can call it
from within another process by using the
[REST Call Action Step](../../rpa-builder/latest/toolbox-network-rest-call).

The second process should take a list of lookup codes provided via a file, use the
[File Controlled Loop Action Step](../../rpa-builder/latest/toolbox-flow-control-file-controlled-loop)
to iterate over all codes, and use the codes within the request body of the REST call.

[Deploy the project](../../rpa-manager/processautomation-deploy#deploy-a-process-automation) and
schedule it as needed.

## Example of Adapting the CRPA File

> [!NOTE] The example in this guide uses real-world data to show you how to adapt a CRPA file to a
> specific AMS.

All values for the Activity Parameters used in RPA Builder are test values that you must overwrite
with your own values when creating the run configuration for the process in RPA Manager.

### Process Model

The process model contains the following activities:

1.  Log in to the AMS using credentials and a database name.
2.  Open a record in the AMS database using a lookup code.
3.  Copy data from various fields on the record pages to a CSV file.

![RPA Builder process model](_images/rpa-setup-process-model.png)

Add activities by clicking on an element on the model panel and choosing an element from the context
menu. For example, the following image shows how to branch off to a second AMS if you are using more
than one AMS and not finding the data you need in the first AMS:

![Add activity to RPA Builder process model](_images/rpa-setup-add-activity.png)

The linter automatically checks the model for completeness and displays error and warning messages
to indicate what to change.

Click on an activity to open the editing workflow.

### Activity 1: Log In to the AMS

#### Overview of the Activity Workflow

1.  Decrypt the AMS login credentials that go in through an Activity Parameter.
2.  Run the AMS.
3.  Log in to the AMS by using the decrypted credentials.
4.  Select the database by using another in-going Activity Parameter.

    ![RPA Builder Activity Parameter Wizard](_images/rpa-setup-activity-parameters-wizard.png)

#### Minimum Steps to Adapt in This Workflow

1.  Change the following in the
    [Run Program Action Step](../../rpa-builder/latest/toolbox-control-run-program):
    1.  Replace the values in the **File name**, **Parameters**, and **Window Title** fields with
        the values from your AMS.
    2.  Click **Test** to run the application so that you can use it to adapt the following Action
        Steps:

        ![RPA Builder Run Program Wizard](_images/rpa-setup-run-program-wizard.png)

2.  Use the **Start from scratch** option to capture and test a new pattern that indicates that the
    login page of the AMS is visible onscreen:

    ![RPA Builder Image Search Wizard](_images/rpa-setup-image-search-wizard.png)

3.  Reidentify the field to enter the password. You might have to add another
    [Keystrokes to App Element Action Step](../../rpa-builder/latest/toolbox-app-automation-keystrokes-to-app-element)
    for entering the user name, which would go before the password step.

    ![RPA Builder Keystrokes to App Element Wizard](_images/rpa-setup-keystrokes-app-element-wizard.png)

4.  Reidentify the **Login** button, which is used in the
    [Click App Element Action Step](../../rpa-builder/latest/toolbox-app-automation-click-app-element):

    ![RPA Builder Click App Element Wizard](_images/rpa-setup-click-app-element-wizard.png)

5.  Wait for the login process to complete by choosing a pattern that disappears after completion:

    ![RPA Builder Image Search Wizard choose pattern](_images/rpa-setup-choose-pattern.png)

6.  Adapt the other Action Steps of the same types in a similar way. Or, add or delete Action Steps
    based on what is needed to log in to your AMS.

### Activity 2: Find a Record in the AMS

The second activity finds a record in the AMS by using a lookup code.

1.  Enter the lookup code, which was provided as an Activity Parameter, as locate criterion to
    search for the account data set.
2.  Click on the result entry to open the account data, and then wait until itâs fully loaded.

![RPA Builder lookup code](_images/rpa-setup-lookup-code.png)

Change the following Action Steps:

1.  Adapt the first Action Steps as you did in the previous Activity.
2.  Adapt the scan area in the
    [AI OCR Action Step](../../rpa-builder/latest/toolbox-text-recognition-ai-ocr-screen-based) in
    which RPA Bot searches for the record entry with the lookup code:

    ![RPA Builder AI OCR screen-based Wizard](_images/rpa-setup-ai-ocr-wizard.png)

3.  Adapt the rest of the Action Steps as you did in the previous Activity.

### Activity 3: Retrieve and Export Data from the AMS

The third activity retrieves data and exports the data from the AMS so that it can be imported to
Salesforce Financial Services Cloud.

1.  Retrieve account data.
2.  Retrieve the policy data of the account.
3.  Export the data to a CSV file.

![RPA Builder export data](_images/rpa-setup-export-data.png)

Change the following Action Steps:

1.  To retrieve the data, reidentify the fields from which to read in each of the
    [Read Text from App Element Action Steps](../../rpa-builder/latest/toolbox-app-automation-read-text-from-app-element)
    and add or delete them as needed.

![RPA Builder Read Text from App Element Wizard](_images/rpa-setup-read-text-app-element-wizard.png)

If necessary, click into a field before reading the text and then use a keystroke to get out of the
field.

![RPA Builder tip for getting in and out of fields](_images/rpa-setup-read-text-tip.png)

To search for text in a special format, you can use
[Optical Character Recognition](../../rpa-builder/latest/toolbox-text-recognition-ai-ocr-screen-based)
in combination with regular expressions.

![How to use optical character recognition in RPA Builder](_images/rpa-setup-optical-character-recognition.png)

2.  Ensure that all retrieved data is [pinned](../../rpa-builder/latest/common-properties#variables)
    to the
    [Combine Strings Action Step](../../rpa-builder/latest/toolbox-variable-handling-combine-strings)
    and adjust if necessary. You can also link more Activity Parameters.

    ![RPA Builder Combine Strings Wizard](_images/rpa-setup-combine-strings-wizard.png)

3.  Add the string to a CSV file as `Pin Variable` and adjust the properties as needed. The CSV file
    must be in the specified format.

    ![RPA Builder Write to Text File Wizard](_images/rpa-setup-write-text-file-wizard.png)

## See Also

- _Salesforce Help_:
  [Account](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/atlas.en-us.object_reference.meta/object_reference/sforce_api_objects_account.htm)
- _Salesforce Help_:
  [Prepare a CSV File for an Import or Update in Salesforce](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=000381876&type=1)
- _Salesforce Help_:
  [Standard Objects](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/atlas.en-us.248.0.object_reference.meta/object_reference/sforce_api_objects_list.htm)
- [MuleSoft Accelerator for Financial Services](./)
---

# Optimize Customer Experiences Use Case Setup Guide

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The
[Optimize Customer Experiences with Data 360](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/mulesoft-accelerator-for-financial-services/minor/1.12/pages/dcb-oxw/Use%20case%205%20-%20Optimize%20customer%20experiences%20with%20Data%20Cloud/)
in MuleSoft Accelerator for Financial Services requires initial configuration in Salesforce
Data 360. Complete the core configuration by following the
[Data 360 learning path](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=data.c360_a_customer360_lm.htm&type=5).

After completing the setup, apply the use case-specific guidance on this page. For additional use
case information and the downloadable assets, refer to the use case documentation in Anypoint
Exchange.

## Data Connectors and Sources

Configure Salesforce CRM and Ingestion API connectors to bring CRM and external system data into
Data 360.

- Salesforce CRM serves as the primary source for Financial Services Cloud customer and account
  data.
- Marketing Cloud is the target destination for audience activation outputs.
- The Ingestion API is used for external data sources such as Snowflake, Databricks, and Amazon S3.

The use case expects source data to include globally unique party identifiers for downstream profile
unification.

## Ingestion API Schema Scope

The ingestion schema includes:

- FinancialAccount
- FinancialTransaction
- ExternalFinancialAccount
- WebEngagement

Refer to the
[FINS Salesforce Data 360 System API Implementation Template](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/fins-salesforce-cdp-sys-api/)
for the Ingestion API schema file and payload examples.

## Data Streams and Mapping Focus

Create the required data streams, and then map source objects to the target data model to make sure
customer profiles and engagement data are segmented and activated.

- Define the streams for the Financial Services objects that are required for profile, transaction,
  and engagement analysis.
- Map source objects to the target model objects that are needed for identity, segmentation, and
  activation outcomes.
- Confirm that required relationships are present before audience publishing.

## Identity Resolution Focus

Configure the identity logic to unify person and account signals that are needed for customer-level
profile views used in personalization and engagement targeting.

## Calculated Insights Focus

Use calculated insights to support audience definitions, campaign decisions, and financial
engagement analytics. For more information, see
[Enhance Data with Insights](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=data.c360_a_insights.htm&type=5).

## Segmentation and Activation Focus

Build audience segments from unified profiles and financial engagement signals. Activate segment
outputs to configured downstream channels, including Marketing Cloud where applicable.

## Guidance for Standalone Deployments

If you deploy the
[FINS Salesforce Data 360 System API](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/org.mule.examples/fins-salesforce-cdp-sys-api/)
independently of the use case, do the following:

- Omit use case-specific segmentation and activation configuration.
- Omit use case-specific Marketing Cloud activation setup.
- Omit use case-specific calculated insights unless required by downstream consumers.
- Keep only the connectors, schemas, streams, and mappings that are required by your API data
  contracts.

## See Also

- [Prerequisites](prerequisites)
- [MuleSoft Accelerator for Financial Services](./)
---

# Salesforce Financial Services Cloud Setup Guide

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

This page describes how to configure an existing Salesforce Financial Services Cloud (FSC) instance
to support the default MuleSoft Accelerator for Financial Services functionality. These instructions
assume that the required FSC packages have been deployed to the target Salesforce instance and that
the appropriate permissions have already been assigned.

## Record Types

The following record types must exist in the corresponding objects for the accelerator assets to
function correctly.

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Salesforce Object</th><th>Record Type</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Account</p></td><td><p>Household</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Financial Account</p></td><td><p>Blockchain Account</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Financial Account</p></td><td><p>General Ledger Account</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Financial Account</p></td><td><p>Money Market Account</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Financial Account Transaction</p></td><td><p>Financial Account Transfer Transaction</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Lead</p></td><td><p>Lead</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Follow the instructions for each object below.

### Account & Lead

To enable the `Household` record type in `Account` object, follow the below steps:

1.  Click the Salesforce Setup icon.
2.  Navigate to `Users -> Profiles`.
3.  Select the target profile for the service account user.
4.  Navigate to `Object Settings -> Accounts` and click the **Edit** button.
5.  Under `Record Types and Page Layout Assignments` check the `Household` record type.
6.  Click the **Save** button.

Follow the same steps but this time enable the `Lead` record type on the `Lead` object.

### Financial Account

To create the `Blockchain Account` record type in the Financial Account object, first create a page
layout for it based on an existing layout.

1.  Select the `Object Manager` tab from the Setup home page.
2.  Select the `FinServ_FinancialAccount__c` object.
3.  Select `Page Layouts` from the left nav and click **New**.
4.  Select an existing page layout (for example, "Financial Account (Savings Account) Layout").
5.  Set the `Page Layout Name` field to "Financial Account (Blockchain Account) Layout".
6.  Click **Save** to create the new layout.

Select `Page Layouts` from the left nav to return to the list and repeat the above steps to create
the `General Ledger Account` and `Money Market Account` page layouts.

Now create the new `Blockchain Account` record type:

1.  Select `Record Type` from the left nav and click **New**.
2.  Select an existing record type (for example, "Savings Account").
3.  Fill out the following fields as indicated.
    - `Record Type Label` -> "Blockchain Account"
    - `Record Type Name` -> "Blockchain" (change default value).
    - `Description` -> "Represents a Blockchain account"

4.  Enable the `Active` checkbox.
5.  In the list of profiles, check the `Make available` option the profile assigned to the target
    API service account (at a minimum).
6.  Click **Next** and select the option `Apply one layout to all profiles`.
7.  Select the page layout "Financial Account (Blockchain Account) Layout".
8.  Click **Save**.

Repeat the above steps to create the `General Ledger Account` and `Money Market Account` record
types. Be sure to select the correct page layout corresponding to the record type.

The new record types should now be available in the `Record Types` section. You can verify that the
new record type is also available to the target user profile as follows:

1.  Navigate to `Users -> Profiles`.
2.  Select the target profile.
3.  Navigate to `Object Settings -> Financial Accounts` (the object you edited earlier) and click
    the **Edit** link.
4.  Verify that the new record types are listed under `Record Types and Page Layout Assignments`.

### Financial Account Transaction

The standard record type **Financial Account Transaction** supports the transaction types
`DebitTransaction` and `CreditTranction`. The **Financial Account Transfer Transaction** record type
must be created to support the `TransferTransaction` and `PaymentTransaction` transaction types.

The steps for creating the `Financial Account Transfer Transaction` record type are similar to those
above, but for the `Financial Transaction` object instead.

1.  In the Object Manager, select the `FinServ_FinancialAccountTransaction__c` object.
2.  Select `Page Layouts` from the left nav and click **New**.
3.  Select an existing page layout (for example, "Financial Account Transaction Layout").
4.  Set the `Page Layout Name` field to "Financial Account Transfer Transaction Layout".
5.  Click **Save**.
6.  Select `Record Types` from the left nav and click **New**.
7.  Select an existing record type (for example, "Financial Account Transaction") if available,
    otherwise use "--Master--".
8.  Fill out the following fields as indicated.
    - `Record Type Label` -> "Financial Account Transfer Transaction".
    - `Record Type Name` -> "Financial_Account_Transfer" (change default value).
    - `Description` -> "Defines a transaction that represents a generic transfer from one account to
      another".

9.  Enable the `Active` checkbox.
10. In the list of profiles, check the `Make available` option the profile assigned to the target
    API service account (at a minimum).
11. Click **Next** and select the option `Apply one layout to all profiles`.
12. Select the page layout "Financial Account Transfer Transaction Layout".
13. Click **Save**.

The new record type can be verified as above.

## Custom Fields

The following fields must be created in the corresponding objects.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Salesforce Object</th><th>Field Label</th><th>Field Name</th><th>Data ID</p></td><td><p>Global_PhoneNumber_Id</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>36</p></td><td><p>Y</p></td><td><p>Y</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Contact</p></td><td><p>Global Mobile Number ID</p></td><td><p>Global_MobileNumber_Id</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>36</p></td><td><p>Y</p></td><td><p>Y</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Contact</p></td><td><p>Global Other Address ID</p></td><td><p>Global_OtherAddress_Id</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>36</p></td><td><p>Y</p></td><td><p>Y</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Contact</p></td><td><p>Global Mailing Address ID</p></td><td><p>Global_MailingAddress_Id</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>36</p></td><td><p>Y</p></td><td><p>Y</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>FinServ_Card__c</p></td><td><p>Card Type</p></td><td><p>Card_Type</p></td><td><p>Picklist</p></td><td><p>-</p></td><td><p>N</p></td><td><p>N</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>FinServ_Card__c</p></td><td><p>Cardholder Name</p></td><td><p>Cardholder_Name__c</p></td><td><p>Text</p></td><td><p>100</p></td><td><p>N</p></td><td><p>N</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>FinServ_Card__c</p></td><td><p>Credit Card Monthly Withdrawals</p></td><td><p>Maximum_Monthly_Withdrawals__c</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td><td><p>18</p></td><td><p>N</p></td><td><p>N</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>FinServ_FinancialAccount__c</p></td><td><p>Monthly Withdrawal Limit</p></td><td><p>Monthly_Withdrawal_Limit__c</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td><td><p>(16,2)</p></td><td><p>N</p></td><td><p>N</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>FinServ_FinancialAccountTransaction__c</p></td><td><p>Debit Financial
